spec:
8-input interrupt priority encoder with masking, edge detection, nested interrupt support, and CPU acknowledgment.

Inputs:
- clk: system clock
- reset_n: active-low async reset
- interrupts[7:0]: 8 interrupt request lines (active high)
- mask_reg[7:0]: mask (bit=1 disables that interrupt)
- current_isr_priority[2:0]: priority of currently-executing ISR (0=highest)
- cpu_ack: pulse high 1 cycle to acknowledge interrupt

Outputs:
- int_valid: high for 1 cycle when a new interrupt is detected
- irq_id[2:0]: ID of highest-priority pending interrupt (0=highest)
- irq_ack[7:0]: one-hot ack, pulsed when cpu_ack asserted with int_valid
- nested_int_pending: high when pending IRQ has higher priority than current ISR
- vector_addr[31:0]: ISR address = 0x00001000 + (irq_id * 4)

Behavior:
- Active interrupts = interrupts AND NOT mask_reg
- Edge detection: only rising edges trigger (compared with previous cycle)
- Fixed priority: IRQ0 highest, IRQ7 lowest
- Simultaneous interrupts: lowest-numbered wins
- int_valid is 1 cycle wide per new edge
- nested_int_pending = int_valid AND (irq_id < current_isr_priority)
- cpu_ack + int_valid produces one-hot irq_ack

Verify: reset clears outputs, single interrupt triggers correctly, masked interrupt ignored, simultaneous priority, edge detection (holding does not re-trigger), nested detection, cpu_ack handshake, vector address calculation.
